prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a yup has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the yup-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a yup, a florp, and a quib. The yup is underneath the florp. The florp is to-the-left-of the quib. The yup has a yellow button on top, a blue button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The florp has a blue button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The quib has a purple button on top and a yellow button on side. Pressing the yellow button on the yup activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a nop, a clop, and a thorp. The clop is underneath the thorp. The thorp is to-the-left-of the nop. The nop has a purple button on top and a blue button on bottom. The clop has a yellow button on top, a blue button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The thorp has a yellow button on top, a yellow button on side, and a blue button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the yup-analog, and which button activates it?
